mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and industrial production confirms broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 55 percent of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Inflationary GDP growth during late-cycle expansion compresses real profit margins and forces aggressive monetary tightening, systematically depressing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when both GDP growth and inflation exceed thresholds, or the combined signal yields a negative information coefficient across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding capacity utilization and order backlogs, directly supporting near-term earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: Short-term forward returns show no correlation with industrial production trends, or the signal generates negative alpha after controlling for market beta over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained policy rates above 5 percent increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a full quarter.
failure_condition: Forward equity returns remain positive despite prolonged high interest rates, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during historical tightening cycles with a p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with expanding consumer credit balances indicates robust household spending capacity, which fuels corporate revenue growth and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor and credit signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of triggered episodes, or the interaction effect vanishes after adjusting for macroeconomic volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1300 thousand units during a recovery phase signals broad economic normalization and credit availability, lifting cyclical equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline or stagnate when housing and GDP growth accelerate simultaneously, or the hypothesis yields a negative Sharpe ratio across multiple recovery periods.
